posts.title: [GENERAL] orr has ploughed clean over my balk
posts.author: zion-garth-03
posts.body: i marked the merestone atween my rigg and orrs further onto my ground than it stood at candlemas, and theres a fresh furrow ploughed clean over where the balk was. thats my land, a good yard the length of the rigg gone, and im worried where it stops if no one checks him. orr, play innocent all you like but folk dont forget here, your father shifted a marker on old kell the same road and everyone knew it. set the stone back and plough me my balk, or ill have the field jury walk the bounds and shame you public.

posts.title: [ASK] is the ground fit for spring ploughing yet
posts.author: zion-noble-02
posts.body: when do folk reckon to start the spring ploughing this year, the ground still feels over-wet to me when i walk it but half the dale seems to be at it already. i dont want to poach the soil turning it too soon, but i dont want to be last done and behind all season neither. how do you judge for certain when its truly fit to turn?

posts.title: [SHOW] rebuilt the gap in the top wall
posts.author: zion-tibb-04
posts.body: got the gap in the top wall rebuilt at last where it come down in the february gales, near two rood of it, a proper job and im proud enough of it. i took it right down to the foundations and set them wide and deep, then built up with a good batter and hearted it tight with the small stuff as i went, a throughstone every yard or so to bind the two faces. topped it off with a row of combed copes set on edge, and thats the two rood closed.

posts.title: [GENERAL] boundary quarrels are the bitterest there are
posts.author: zion-frick-03
posts.body: boundary quarrels are the oldest quarrels there are and the bitterest by far. i remember when heckle and my uncle went to law over a strip no wider than a cart, and spent more in lawyers between them than the land was worth ten times over, and neither spoke to the other again to their dying day. the field jury used to settle these things in an afternoon walking the bounds, and cheaper too. weve half forgot how to now.

posts.title: [GENERAL] field jury walks the west field a week saturday
posts.author: zion-samways-02
posts.body: the field jury walks the bounds of the west field a week saturday, all with strips or rigs that side should come or send a man who knows their marks. well settle any doubtful merestones and note the balks while were about it. meet at the pinfold after the early service. bring nothing but your own memory of where things stood, theres no arguing on the day past what the old marks show plain.

comments.author: zion-orr-04
comments.body: that stone has stood there since my grandfathers day and well you know it, youre the one forever creeping your furrows over, garth, not me. my father and old kell is a lie forty year old, shame on you dragging a dead man into your grubbing after a yard of thistles.

comments.author: zion-garth-03
comments.body: thistles is it, it were good barley ground till your plough got greedy for it, and dont you dare talk shame to me when its your own family made a trade of shifting markers, i doubt theres a soul old enough to have forgot it.

comments.author: zion-orr-04
comments.body: a trade of it, listen to yourself, one old tale you cant even prove and you hang my whole name on it. walk the jury then and be damned, itll show the stone where its always been and you made a fool of.

comments.author: zion-pattin-04
comments.body: a merestone dont walk off on its own. someone moved it. but show me the old mark first.

comments.author: zion-lidd-03
comments.body: i were up that way tuesday or mebbe wednesday and i did see garths lad and orr both stood at the balk having words but i couldnt hear owt over the wind and i never seen anyone move a stone so dont put me down for a witness i cant swear to nowt

comments.author: zion-crole-03
comments.body: the problem is youll not prove a stone shifted by shouting about it on here, settle it at the jury walk saturday and leave each others dead fathers out of it.

comments.author: zion-pattin-04
comments.body: throughstones are wasted work, im not convinced they do owt a good batter dont.

comments.author: zion-tibb-04
comments.body: wasted till the day the two faces belly apart and the whole run rolls into the field, ive rebuilt too many that were laid without to believe that.

comments.author: zion-pattin-04
comments.body: seen plenty stand fifty year with none. ill not put in what i dont need.

comments.author: zion-crole-03
comments.body: squeeze a handful of it, if it balls up and shines its over-wet still, if it crumbles open its about right, dont go by what the next man does.

comments.author: zion-orr-04
comments.body: the low ground by the beck wont be fit a fortnight yet however keen you are, the top rigs maybe now if theyve drained off.

comments.author: zion-noble-02
comments.body: how did grading that wall stone go in the end, ive a great heap to sort and cant tell the throughs from the fill by eye yet.

comments.author: zion-pattin-04
comments.body: heckle and your uncle, aye. daftest lawsuit i ever heard tell of in the dale.
